exemplar.title: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains clear and sustained focus on the prescribed title, explicitly defining key terms (âalways,â âever-improving tools,â âimproved knowledgeâ).
Effectively links arguments to two areas of knowledge (human sciences and natural sciences) using discipline-specific examples (BSA surveys, AI in economics, neutrino detection, Thalidomide scandal).
Presents coherent arguments that are generally supported by examples.
Demonstrates awareness of alternative perspectives, acknowledging limits of tools, knower dependence, and unintended consequences.
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive with limited depth of evaluation; counter-positions are addressed only at a surface level.
No explicit formulation of guiding knowledge questions, reducing conceptual rigor.
Transitions between sections are sometimes abrupt, affecting overall coherence.
Conclusion summarizes rather than critically weighs implications or synthesizes key examples to drive home the overarching argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates the qualities of a band 5â6 response: sustained focus on the title, effective linkage to areas of knowledge, coherent use of examples, and some acknowledgment of differing viewpoints. However, the studentâs treatment of evidence is often descriptive rather than analytical, counterarguments are under-developed, and there is no explicit guiding knowledge question to sharpen the inquiry. Abrupt transitions and a summary-heavy conclusion further limit depth. These strengths and limitations justify placing the essay solidly within the 5â6 band and awarding 6 out of 10.

annotations.comment: The thesis statement in the opening paragraph outlines scope but lacks a precise claim or roadmap of arguments. The student should state a concise position on âalwaysâ to provide a clear structure for the subsequent discussion.

The assertion that knowledge in the human sciences is purely objective oversimplifies epistemological debates. The student should acknowledge interpretivist perspectives that challenge the notion of complete objectivity.
The discussion of unintended consequences is compelling but remains descriptive. The student should engage with counterarguments that tools may sometimes constrain ignorance, not exacerbate it.

There is a missed opportunity to reflect on how the Thalidomide scandal changed testing protocols. Assessing the long-term impact on scientific standards would illustrate deeper implications of tool improvement.

This concluding reflection on unintended consequences introduces a valuable insight but is not fully developed. The student should connect this back to the idea of âalwaysâ in the prescribed title.

The overall structure occasionally shifts abruptly between human sciences and natural sciences. To improve coherence, the student should use clear signposting or transition sentences to guide the reader through sections.
The argument regarding tool capacity is conceptually sound but would benefit from a concrete case study demonstrating how missed variables altered conclusions in human sciences research.
The Thalidomide example effectively illustrates knower ignorance but could be strengthened by discussing the ethical responsibilities of researchers and how these intersect with tool use.
The student claims polarimeters were readily available but does not consider institutional or regulatory reasons for non-use. Analysing these systemic factors would deepen the evaluation.

The introduction effectively defines key terms but does not present an explicit knowledge question to guide the inquiry. The student should formulate a clear guiding question (e.g. âTo what extentâ¦â) to sharpen the essayâs conceptual focus and enhance analytical depth.

The discussion of bias reduction remains descriptive. To deepen analysis, the student could critique potential unintended biases introduced by weighting systems and consider their ethical implications.

The description of the BSAâs sampling improvements is informative but lacks evaluation of how these changes concretely affect data validity and the reliability of findings. The student should analyse the impact on knowledge quality.

The example on multi-stage sampling is effective but the student should link this technical improvement back to the essayâs title by assessing whether tool refinements necessarily produce improved knowledge âalwaysâ or only in certain contexts.
The transition to the counterargument is abrupt and undeveloped. The student should expand on how tool over-reliance specifically undermines knowledge, using a concrete example in human sciences.

The quote from Andrew Keen highlights the amateur-expert tension but lacks a clear example of amateur content leading to knowledge distortion. Providing specific case studies would strengthen the critique.

The discussion of AI and machine learning introduces an important tool but does not examine how model biases may evolve despite improvements. The student should assess shifts in algorithmic bias over time.

The neutrino detection example is historically rich but its direct relevance to âever-improving tools always resulting in improved knowledgeâ could be clarified by discussing detector sensitivity thresholds.
There is no analysis of alternative interpretations of the neutrino data or of how initial false negatives may have shaped scientific understanding. Including this would add evaluative depth.

The explanation omits discussion of how subsequent detector innovations may introduce new uncertainties (e.g. systematic errors), which is essential to evaluating whether improvements always yield better knowledge.
The assertion that problem-solving drives new knowledge assumes causal certainty. The student should evaluate cases where renewed problem-solving led to flawed or overturned understandings.

The link between solving violations of conservation laws and knowledge improvement is asserted rather than analysed. The student should critically examine whether early neutrino models contained conceptual gaps despite detection advances.
The conclusion accurately summarizes arguments but does not critically weigh which perspective carries greater explanatory power. The student should explicitly evaluate the strongest counterclaim.

The final statement articulates a two-way relationship between tools and knowers but lacks propositions for future research. Including potential knowledge questions for further exploration would strengthen closure.

The conclusion uses general language without referencing specific examples from earlier sections. The student should synthesize key cases (e.g. BSA, neutrino, Thalidomide) to reinforce the overarching argument.

The essay frequently restates that tools alone do not guarantee improved knowledge but stops short of offering criteria for when improvements are most effective. Defining such conditions would add conceptual rigor.

The essay does not

on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
